qa_pairs.question: What is the primary goal of this document?
qa_pairs.answer: This document aims to describe how to migrate virtual machine (VM) instances from the legacy Arbutus Cloud to the new Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: When is the legacy Arbutus Cloud scheduled for decommissioning?
qa_pairs.answer: The legacy Arbutus Cloud will be decommissioned in 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What is the migration deadline for RAS and RAC Projects between Legacy and New Arbutus Clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline for both RAS and RAC Projects to migrate between Legacy Arbutus and New Arbutus is August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to migrate cloud resources from the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Migration is necessary for all cloud resources on the legacy Arbutus Cloud because the legacy Arbutus Cloud will be decommissioned in 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for choosing the migration approach?
qa_pairs.answer: You and your research team need to select the migration approach(es) appropriate for your research project.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for questions about a migration plan or to review it?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions or wish to review your migration plan with an Arbutus Cloud team member, please contact cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What types of cloud resources require migration from the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: All cloud resources (e.g. instances, storage volumes, object storage containers, networks, keys, etc.) on the legacy Arbutus Cloud require migration.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first question to answer when planning a cloud migration from legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to determine which resources in your legacy Arbutus cloud project need migration, noting that not all resources may require it.

qa_pairs.question: Should I migrate all resources from the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Not all resources may require migration. For example, if any volume or an instance is no longer needed, it could be decommissioned instead of being migrated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the difference between ephemeral and volume-backed instances?
qa_pairs.answer: Volume-backed instances boot from a volume (i.e. /dev/vda) and additionally may have other volumes attached, while ephemeral instances do not boot from a volume.

qa_pairs.question: How should volumes larger than 150 GB be migrated?
qa_pairs.answer: Volumes larger than 150 GB should be migrated using Globus.

qa_pairs.question: If I've used an automated deployment system on legacy Arbutus Cloud, how should I proceed with migration?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have used automation (e.g., Terraform, Ansible), your automation tools should be used in the process of migration.

qa_pairs.question: Will custom DNS entries need to be updated during the migration process?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, custom DNS entries will need to be updated because your IP addresses will change as the new Arbutus Cloud uses different floating IP address ranges than the legacy Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Which interface should I use for simple migrations on Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Simple migrations can be completed via the OpenStack Dashboard (i.e. the Horizon Web UI).

qa_pairs.question: Which interface might be necessary for more complex migrations on Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: More complex migrations may require OpenStack Command Line Interface (CLI) access.

qa_pairs.question: Is OpenStack account sharing allowed?
qa_pairs.answer: No, account sharing is strictly forbidden. Any person who requires an account should apply here: https://www.alliancecan.ca/en/our-services/advanced-research-computing/account-management/apply-account.

qa_pairs.question: How long can a migration outage last?
qa_pairs.answer: Depending on the scope of what needs to be migrated, an outage could range from a couple of hours to a couple of days.

qa_pairs.question: What is required if I have a RAS project that needs migration?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have a RAS project, you will need to submit a request to migrate your project to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do after completing my migration from Legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Once you have completed your migration, please submit a ticket to request your project on Legacy Arbutus be decommissioned.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Horizon Web UI URL for the Legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the Legacy Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI is https://arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Horizon Web UI URL for the New Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the New Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI is https://arbutus.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Which web browsers are supported for accessing the Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI?
qa_pairs.answer: Firefox and Chrome browsers are supported, while Safari and Edge may work but have not been validated.

qa_pairs.question: Will my cloud project, network, and router be set up automatically in the New Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, your Arbutus Cloud Project (Tenant), Network, and Router will be pre-created for you in Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Will new Security Groups be needed in the New Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: New Security Groups (OpenStack’s firewall rules) may be required because the floating IP range in the New Arbutus Cloud is different than the Legacy Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What is the most important preliminary step before migrating instances?
qa_pairs.answer: The most important preliminary step is to back up any critical data, as the Arbutus Cloud Team does not make backups or copies of instances.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for creating backups of critical data before migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Project owners are responsible for creating backups which may be necessary for your migration, as the Arbutus Cloud Team does not make backups or copies of instances.
